question_text: A student creates waves on a rope by oscillating one end. She counts 12 complete waves along a 3-metre section of rope and observes that 4 waves pass a fixed point every 2 seconds. Calculate the speed of the waves on the rope.
explanation: First, find the wavelength: 12 waves in 3 m means wavelength = 3 ÷ 12 = 0.25 m. Next, find frequency: 4 waves in 2 seconds means 2 waves per second, so frequency = 2 Hz. Finally, use speed = frequency × wavelength = 2 × 0.25 = 0.5 m/s. (Correction: 3 ÷ 12 = 0.25 m, 4 waves ÷ 2 sec = 2 Hz, so speed = 2 × 0.25 = 0.5 m/s.) Wait—recalculating: wavelength is 3m/12 waves = 0.25m. If 4 waves pass in 2 seconds, frequency = 4/2 = 2 Hz. Speed = 2 Hz × 0.25 m = 0.5 m/s.
